turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Hello, CityWheels car-share, Gemma speaking. How can I help?

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: Hi. A colleague recommended your scheme, and I'd like to sign up so I can borrow a car now and then without owning one.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: You've come to the right place. I can take your details and get your application started. Could I have your full name?

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: It's Daniel Bradshaw. The surname's B-R-A-D-S-H-A-W.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Thank you. And roughly how did you hear about us — I like to note it. You said a colleague?

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: Yes, a colleague at work. Though I'd also seen a poster at the station, to be honest.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: I'll put colleague, since that came first. Now, membership requires a valid driving licence. Could you read me the licence number? It's the long line on your photocard.

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: Certainly. It's BRADS, then nine-oh-six-two-five, then D-J-nine-A-A. So BRADS906 25DJ9AA.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Let me read that back — BRADS906 25DJ9AA. And how long have you held your licence?

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: About seven years now. No penalty points or anything like that.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: That's ideal — we do ask for a minimum of two years, so you're well clear. Now, there are two membership plans. The Occasional plan has no monthly fee, but the hourly rate is a bit higher, at seven pounds an hour. The Frequent plan is five pounds an hour, but with a monthly fee of eight pounds. Which suits you?

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: I think I'll only use it a couple of times a month, so the Occasional plan makes more sense for me — no monthly fee.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Sensible choice. There's also a one-off joining fee of twenty pounds, and a refundable deposit. Now — which is your nearest car park? We keep cars at three locations: the Market Square, the Riverside car park, and the one on Chapel Street.

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: The Riverside one would be handiest — oh, hang on, actually Chapel Street is closer to my flat. Let me change that to Chapel Street.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Chapel Street, no problem. Once you're approved, we'll post you a smart membership card. You tap it on the windscreen to unlock the car, and the keys are kept inside the glovebox.

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: And how do I actually book a car?

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Everything's done through the app. You can book right up to the last minute, and you can extend your booking too, as long as no one's reserved the car after you. One rule people forget — please return the car with at least a quarter of a tank of fuel. There's a fuel card in the glovebox, so you never pay for petrol yourself.

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: Good to know. What if I bring it back late?

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: Late returns are charged at double the hourly rate, and it can disrupt the next member, so we're quite strict about that. But give us a call if you're delayed and we'll do our best to help. Your card should arrive within about five working days.

turns.speaker: Daniel
turns.text: That's everything I needed. Thanks so much, Gemma.

turns.speaker: Gemma
turns.text: You're welcome, Daniel. Welcome aboard — you'll have your card by the end of the week.

The membership card should arrive within about ______ working days.
